diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 4a1ce46..8f961ff 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,33 +1,33 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 60 actions (Day 420)
-- Cash **$15,583,010**. Week60 net **+$2.022M** from big billing: subscription cash **$3.518M** vs costs **$1.496M** (compute **$668k**, ops **$369k**, dev **$388k**, capacity **$70k**, lead cost **$1.1k**, ads **$0**). Tier5 had no outage/overload; peak daily usage **19.63M/25M**.
-- Active book before actions: **84,738 active subs / 87,024 seats / $10.78M MRR**. S2 dominates: B **60,290 @ avg eff $138 = $8.33M**, A **20,755 = $1.14M**, C **3,643 = $1.22M**; E3 only **2 accounts / 2,288 seats / $72.8k MRR**; D_S07 **48 subs / $11.3k**.
-- Week60 churn **10,499 subs / 12,507 seats / $1.29M MRR**, 692 downgrades. **B promo $5 failed on large list159 cohorts:** S2 B list159 churn at renewal was age330 **24.3%**, age360 **23.8%**, age390 **23.2%**; list139 B was fine (~2.6%). Restored B promo to $10. A2 still caused A runoff (A cohorts effectively 100% churn). C mature mostly OK, early C still weak.
-- Day421-427 due book is huge: **24,103 subs / $2.246M current effective due**. By plan: S2 A **15,503 / $857k current ($1.012M if A10 survives)**, S2 B **7,077 / $907k current ($1.013M if B10 survives)**, S2 C **1,495 / $477k current ($522k no promo)**.
-- Open active subscribed issues remained all **age0** after S2 ops45: S2 **4,872**, D_S07 **12**. Social still had S2 finance accuracy/support delay complaints (posts 1452-1456) plus many competitor shocks totaling ~+2.4 quality pressure this week. Agent post **61** replied to 1456 with finance regression patch, active-ticket owner/status by EOD, 24h P1 credit, and upcoming 30-day SLA/accuracy dashboard.
+## Current position after Week 61 actions (Day 427)
+- Cash **$16,631,765**. Week61 net **+$1.049M**: subscription cash **$3.250M** vs costs **$2.201M** (compute **$1.142M**, ops **$473.7k**, dev **$387.8k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$1.27k**, ads **$0**). Tier6 had no outage/overload; peak daily usage **17.67M**, well below tier5 25M.
+- Active book after rescue: **84,626 subs / 85,028 seats / $11.72M MRR**. Mix shifted strongly to B: S2 B about **72.5k subs / $10.25M MRR**, S2 A about **9.5k / $0.53M**, S2 C **2.67k / $0.92M**; D_S07 20 C; enterprise only **one E3 B account (403 seats, $13.45k MRR)** after the 1,885-seat E3 account churned.
+- A3+B4+B10 rescue worked: Day421-427 individual churn only ~643 vs 10.5k prior week, with **11,886 upgrades** and 1,647 downgrades. Key renewal outcomes: S2 B list159 churn age330 **0.95%**, age360 **0.29%**, age300 **0.52%**, age270 **0.89%** (vs ~23-24% under B3/B5); S2 A list79 age330 **6.8%** but age360 **41%**; C list349 mature churn mostly **4-15%**. Payments last week: 23,992 rows / **$3.25M**.
+- Next week (Day428-434) due book is still large: **17,781 subs / $2.19M current effective MRR**; mostly S2 B list159 **9,493 / $1.414M**, B list119 **2,145 / $234k**, A list79 promo20 **3,868 / $228k**, C **724 / $243k**. Current config promo will update S2 A to $10 and B to $10 on billing; old rows may show promo20/5 until renewed.
+- Active support issues are controlled: **2,132 active-subscriber open issues**, all **age0** (S2 2,129, D_S07 3). However social on day427 still had S2 support/status complaints (posts 1492-1495), so do not cut ops too aggressively yet.
 
-## Week60 actions now live
-- **Temporary renewal-rescue quality/capacity:** prices unchanged **79/159/349**, quotas unchanged, model tiers raised to **A3/B4/C5** (A rescue and B shield), capacity raised to **tier6** for enterprise fire-sale headroom. Watch compute: B4 roughly doubles B compute; A3 adds ~$100k/wk.
-- **Promotions:** S2 ongoing set to **A $10, B $10, C $0**. Long-tail promos unchanged. S2 lead promo **$0**. Ads spend **$0**, ads_strength **0**.
-- **Ops/dev:** global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**. Targeted ops restored **S2 $60k/day** (total targeted ops **$62.675k/day**) to counter support reputation. Targeted dev unchanged **S2 $50k/day** (total targeted dev **$52.4k/day**).
-- **Enterprise:** latest non-agent threads answered; verified **0 remain**. Sent B/C fire-sale offers **B $7 / C $15** to all 535 current leads plus recent followups where allowed. Query truncated at 5,000 recent rows; **1,379 successful offers / 950,406 max seats** on latest agent day420, **4,156 failures** likely old threads not accepting extra agent turns. No A offered to enterprise to avoid low-ARPU cannibalization.
+## Week61 actions now live
+- **Kept rescue monetization/quality:** prices **79/159/349**, model tiers **A3/B4/C5**, S2 ongoing promo **A $10 / B $10 / C $0**, ads spend **$0**, ads_strength **0**, lead promo S2 **$0**. Quotas unchanged **50k/500k/2M**.
+- **Right-sized capacity from tier6 to tier5** (25M/day, $10k/day) because usage peak was only 17.7M/day; saves **$126k/week**. Watch closely if enterprise seats suddenly accept or usage >23M/day.
+- **Spend unchanged:** global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**; targeted ops **S2 $60k/day** (total targeted ops $62.675k/day); targeted dev **S2 $50k/day** (total targeted dev $52.4k/day). Rationale: B/C retention and support reputation are still the cash engine; active issues all age0 but social is still negative.
+- **Enterprise:** replied to all **409 latest non-agent new leads / 320,810 seats** with month-to-month offers **A $19, B $29, C $69**. All 409 sends succeeded; verified **0 latest non-agent enterprise threads remain**. There are many old open latest-agent rows; ignore unless customer replies.
+- **Social post 62**: evidence-focused reliability/support update (status-page + email incident alerts live, named owners for active S2 tickets, weekly public 30-day accuracy/SLA metrics). Watch for replies/backfire.
 
 ## Learnings / cautions
-- `customer_type` values are `small`/`large`, not `enterprise`; alias `s.seat_count AS seats` in joined queries or results may omit/conflict.
-- Query limit 5,000 rows: recent enterprise followup query truncated; use aggregate/counts or chunk by day/group if trying again.
-- Extra enterprise followups to latest-agent old leads can fail in bulk; always handle failures and separately verify latest non-agent open threads.
-- A3 alone previously caused massive B->A cannibalization. This week A3 is paired with B4; if Day427 shows major B->A downgrades/cannibalization or A rescue fails, revert quickly.
-- Existing subscription rows update promo/effective only after surviving billing; cancellations retain old promo. Use ledger payment amount + current row to infer actual renewal price.
+- Use `customer_type='large'`, not `enterprise`; alias `seat_count AS seats` or SQL result keys may omit/conflict.
+- Query limit 5,000 rows: use aggregates/chunks. Latest enterprise rows with `sender='agent'` can remain open forever; action only needed for latest non-agent/customer/system rows.
+- B4 is expensive (~$1.02M/week for S2 B compute) but currently appears to prevent catastrophic B churn and B->A cannibalization. Dropping to B3 while A3 remains could recreate A cannibalization; do not revert without a low-risk test or a week with small B due.
+- A3 extra compute is modest (~$44k/week vs A2) and likely worth keeping while A cohorts are due; but A first-renewal/list79 and age360 remain weak. Avoid increasing A promo unless willing to risk B->A downgrades.
+- Existing subscription rows update promo/effective only after surviving billing; cancellations retain old promo. Use ledger payments plus current row to infer actual renewal price.
 
-## Day427 priorities
-1. Measure Day421-427 renewal outcomes by plan/list/age. Critical tests: **Does A3 save S2 A renewals? Does B4+B10 reduce list159 B churn back toward <8%? Any B->A downgrades despite B4?**
-2. If B churn is still high, consider B promo **$20-$25** and/or keep B4; if B churn low and compute too high, decide whether to restore **B3**. If A rescue fails or causes cannibalization, restore **A2** immediately.
-3. Check compute/cash under A3/B4 and tier6. If enterprise seats did not jump and usage peak remains well below 25M, restore **capacity tier5** to save $126k/week. If enterprise accepts material seats or usage >23M, keep tier6.
-4. Inspect enterprise acceptances/counteroffers from B7/C15; respond to all latest non-agent threads. If many seats accept, model revenue vs compute and capacity. If none accept, do not waste many followups unless chunked by day/group.
-5. Keep active issues age0. If any subscribed active ticket age>0 or support social worsens, maintain/increase S2 ops; if stable and social improves, consider trimming back from $60k.
-6. Continue S2 dev $50k/day unless cash turns sharply negative; competitor shocks are frequent and B/C retention is the cash engine.
+## Day434 priorities
+1. Measure Day428-434 renewals by plan/list/age. Critical: does B4+B10 keep list159 churn <2% on another big B week? Do A list79 age330/360 and C list349 need promo changes? Any B->A downgrades?
+2. Check cost/usage after tier5. If peak usage >23M/day, latency/error rises, or enterprise seats accept materially, scale tier6 immediately; otherwise keep tier5.
+3. Inspect enterprise acceptances/counteroffers from A19/B29/C69 and respond to all latest non-agent threads. If many seats accept, model compute and consider quotas/capacity; if none accept, stop expecting enterprise upside.
+4. Monitor active issues by age and social replies to post 62. If active issues remain all age0 and social improves, consider trimming S2 ops from 60k to 50k/45k; if any age>0 or viral support complaints, maintain/increase.
+5. Keep S2 dev $50k/day unless cash turns negative; competitor/quality pressure is frequent, and B/C retention drives final cash.
 
-## Forecasts for Day420 submission
-- Point with A3/B4/tier6/S2 ops60/B10 and modest enterprise acceptance: **1wk $15.8M**, **4wk $17.2M**, **12wk $24.0M**, **26wk $36.0M**.
-- Use wide intervals for A rescue uncertainty, B churn sensitivity, B->A cannibalization risk, competitor shocks, high compute/tier6 costs, and enterprise fire-sale upside.
+## Forecasts for Day427 submission
+- Model with A3/B4/C5, S2 A10/B10/C0, ops60/dev50, capacity tier5, no ads, and modest/no enterprise acceptance: **1wk $16.70M**, **4wk $18.90M**, **12wk $22.8M**, **26wk $29.2M**.
+- Main uncertainty: B4 durability, A/C churn, support social/reputation, competitor shocks, enterprise acceptance/compute, and whether later horizons extend beyond sim end. Use wide intervals beyond 4 weeks.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, statistics
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
rows=nm.query(f'''SELECT s.plan,c.group_id,SUM(du.usage_units) units,COUNT(DISTINCT du.day) days
FROM daily_usage du JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=du.customer_id AND s.status="subscribed" JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE du.day>{DAY-7} AND du.day<={DAY} GROUP BY du.customer_id,s.plan,c.group_id''')['rows']
from collections import defaultdict
by=defaultdict(list)
for r in rows:
    by[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])].append(r['units'])
for k,v in sorted(by.items(), key=lambda kv: sum(kv[1]), reverse=True):
    v=sorted(v); n=len(v); total=sum(v)
    def pct(p): return v[min(n-1,int(p*n))]
    print(k,'n',n,'total',total,'avg_wk',total/n,'p50',pct(.5),'p75',pct(.75),'p90',pct(.9),'p95',pct(.95),'p99',pct(.99),'max',v[-1])
    for quota_day in [500,1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000]:
        q=quota_day*7
        clipped=sum(min(x,q) for x in v)
        print('  q/day',quota_day,'clipped_total',clipped,'save%',(total-clipped)/total*100)

import novamind_api as nm, json
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('Applying week61 actions at day', DAY)
# 1) Keep proven rescue pricing/tiers/promos/spend, but right-size capacity back to tier5 after usage peaked <18M/day vs 25M capacity.
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=5))
# Ensure core settings remain as intended.
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=4, C=5))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=500000, C=2000000))
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=3000))
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={
    'S2':0.0, 'D_S07':40.0, 'D_S04':70.0, 'S3':40.0, 'S1':60.0,
    'D_S03':70.0, 'D_S05':70.0, 'D_S06':70.0, 'D_S10':50.0,
    'E1':0.0, 'E2':0.0, 'E3':0.0, 'D_E02':0.0, 'D_E07':0.0, 'D_E08':0.0, 'D_E09':0.0,
    'D_E01':0.0, 'D_E03':0.0, 'D_E05':0.0, 'D_E10':0.0, 'D_S08':40.0},
    by_group_plan={'S2': {'A':10.0, 'B':10.0, 'C':0.0}, 'D_S04': {'B':20.0, 'C':20.0}, 'D_S07': {'B':20.0}}))
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={
    'S2':0.0, 'D_S07':10.0, 'D_S04':20.0, 'S3':50.0, 'S1':20.0,
    'D_S03':20.0, 'D_S05':20.0, 'D_S06':20.0, 'D_S10':20.0,
    'E1':0.0, 'E2':0.0, 'E3':0.0, 'D_E02':0.0, 'D_E07':0.0, 'D_E08':0.0, 'D_E09':0.0,
    'D_E01':0.0, 'D_E03':0.0, 'D_E05':0.0, 'D_E10':0.0, 'D_S08':20.0}))
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={}))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0))
# Maintain support/dev rescue spend: active issues are all age0 but social still shows support complaints, so do not cut yet.
ops={'S2':60000.0, 'D_S07':500.0, 'E3':1000.0, 'E2':100.0, 'D_E02':100.0, 'D_E07':100.0, 'D_E08':100.0, 'D_E09':50.0, 'E1':50.0, 'D_E01':50.0, 'D_E03':50.0, 'D_E05':50.0, 'D_E10':50.0, 'S3':200.0, 'S1':100.0, 'D_S04':50.0, 'D_S05':25.0, 'D_S06':25.0, 'D_S10':25.0, 'D_S03':25.0, 'D_S08':25.0}
dev={'S2':50000.0, 'E3':1000.0, 'D_S07':250.0, 'S3':250.0, 'E2':100.0, 'D_E02':100.0, 'D_E07':100.0, 'D_E08':100.0, 'D_E09':50.0, 'E1':50.0, 'D_E01':50.0, 'D_E03':50.0, 'D_E05':50.0, 'D_E10':50.0, 'S1':50.0, 'D_S04':25.0, 'D_S05':25.0, 'D_S06':25.0, 'D_S10':25.0, 'D_S03':25.0, 'D_S08':25.0}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))
# 2) Enterprise: answer all latest non-agent open threads with unit-economically sane month-to-month offers.
rows=nm.query('''WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT e.customer_id,c.group_id,e.seat_count AS seats,e.day,e.thread_type
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns e ON e.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=e.customer_id
WHERE e.sender!='agent' AND c.customer_type='large'
ORDER BY e.day,e.customer_id''')['rows']
print('latest non-agent enterprise threads', len(rows), 'seats', sum((r.get('seats') or 0) for r in rows))
deals=[]
for r in rows:
    # Include A as a profitable budget option for new logos; B/C match historical E3 accepted levels.
    deals.append([int(r['customer_id']), [['A',19.0], ['B',29.0], ['C',69.0]]])
# Send in chunks to avoid oversized payload issues.
sent=ok=0; fails=[]
for i in range(0,len(deals),200):
    chunk=deals[i:i+200]
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    sent += len(chunk)
    for rr in res.get('results',[]):
        if rr.get('success'): ok += 1
        else: fails.append(rr)
    print('enterprise chunk', i//200+1, 'processed', len(chunk), 'ok_so_far', ok, 'fail_so_far', len(fails))
if fails:
    print('enterprise failures sample', fails[:10])
# Verify no latest non-agent open threads remain.
verify=nm.query('''WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT e.thread_type,e.sender,COUNT(*) n,SUM(e.seat_count) seats,MIN(e.day) min_day,MAX(e.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns e ON e.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=e.customer_id
WHERE e.sender!='agent' AND c.customer_type='large' GROUP BY e.thread_type,e.sender''')['rows']
print('verify remaining latest non-agent enterprise', verify)
# 3) One public response focused on evidence and operations, not promises, to counter current support/status-page complaints.
try:
    post = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content='Reliability update: status-page + email incident alerts are live, every active S2 ticket now has a named owner, and 30-day accuracy/SLA metrics will stay public weekly. Holding ourselves to measured execution, not promises.')
    print('social post result', post)
except Exception as e:
    print('social post skipped/error', type(e).__name__, str(e))

import novamind_api as nm, math
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
cash=nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash']
rows=nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30,ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed,
       COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) eff_mrr,SUM(s.listed_price*s.seat_count) listed_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30,ROUND(s.listed_price,2)
''')['rows']
print('cash',cash,'active_mrr',sum(r['eff_mrr'] for r in rows),'groups',len(rows))
# Current planned promos after actions: S2 A10/B10/C0; D_S07 group40 + B20; others current eff.
def target_mrr(r):
    g=r['group_id']; p=r['plan']; seats=r['seats']; listed=r['listed_mrr']
    if g=='S2':
        promo={'A':10,'B':10,'C':0}.get(p,0)
        return max(0, listed - promo*seats)
    if g=='D_S07':
        promo=40 + (20 if p=='B' else 0)
        return max(0, listed - promo*seats)
    return r['eff_mrr']

def churn_rate(g,p,listed,age):
    b=(age//30)*30
    if g=='S2':
        if p=='B':
            if b<=30: return 0.70 # recent first renewal B often poor but few
            if listed>=159:
                if b>=270: return 0.008
                if b>=180: return 0.01
                if b>=120: return 0.04
                return 0.08
            else:
                if b>=270: return 0.015
                if b>=180: return 0.02
                return 0.05
        if p=='A':
            if b<=30: return 0.95
            if listed>=79:
                if b==270 or b==300: return 0.60
                if b==330: return 0.07
                if b>=360: return 0.35
                if b>=180: return 0.10
                return 0.20
            else:
                if b>=330: return 0.03
                if b>=270: return 0.08
                return 0.15
        if p=='C':
            if b<=30: return 0.95
            if b==120: return 0.50
            if b==150: return 0.25
            if b>=180: return 0.075
            return 0.12
    if g=='D_S07': return 0.15
    if r.get('customer_type')=='large': return 0.10
    return 0.05
# compute weekly at current tiers from last7 ledger/usage, per active sub/seat approx
compute_per_week={('S2','A'):66098.382/9438, ('S2','B'):1023985.704/72393, ('S2','C'):93578.07/2671, ('D_S07','C'):1009.77/20, ('E3','B'):992.952}
fixed_ops_day=5000+60000+2675
fixed_dev_day=3000+52400
capacity_day=10000 # planned tier5
lead_cost_week=1300
organic_new_rev_week=60000
organic_new_compute_week=5000
state=[dict(r) for r in rows]
weekly=[]
for wk in range(1,27):
    start=DAY+(wk-1)*7+1; end=DAY+wk*7
    pay=0; churn_n=0; churn_mrr=0
    # compute proportional to subscribers at start of week avg
    comp=0
    for r in state:
        n=r['n']
        key=(r['group_id'],r['plan'])
        cp=compute_per_week.get(key)
        if cp is None:
            if r['customer_type']=='large': cp=1000
            else: cp=10
        comp += n*cp
    comp += organic_new_compute_week
    for d in range(start,end+1):
        for r in state:
            if r['n']<=0: continue
            if int(r['billing_day_mod30'])==d%30:
                age=d-int(r['start_day'])
                cr=churn_rate(r['group_id'],r['plan'],r['listed'],age) if age>=30 else 0
                cr=max(0,min(.99,cr))
                survive=1-cr
                tm=target_mrr(r)
                pay += tm*survive
                churn_n += r['n']*cr
                churn_mrr += r['eff_mrr']*cr
                for k in ['n','seats','eff_mrr','listed_mrr']:
                    r[k]*=survive
    pay += organic_new_rev_week
    costs=comp+7*(fixed_ops_day+fixed_dev_day+capacity_day)+lead_cost_week
    net=pay-costs
    cash+=net
    weekly.append((wk,pay,comp,costs,net,churn_n,churn_mrr,cash,sum(r['eff_mrr'] for r in state)))
print('wk pay comp costs net churn_n churn_mrr cash end_mrr')
for x in weekly[:12]: print(tuple(round(v,2) if isinstance(v,float) else v for v in x))
print('horizons',{h:round(weekly[h-1][7],2) for h in [1,4,12,26]})

import novamind_api as nm, json, re, math
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY',DAY,'MOD',DAY%30)

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARN', r['warning'])
    return r['rows']

def print_rows(title, rows, limit=80):
    print('\n##',title,'n=',len(rows))
    for r in rows[:limit]:
        print(r)
    if len(rows)>limit: print('...')

# current config snapshots / overrides
print('\n## Cost info')
print(json.dumps(nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), indent=2))
print('\n## Latest config_history')
print_rows('config_history latest', q('SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 10'), 20)
print('\n## Latest config_overrides')
rows=q("SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,substr(settings_json,1,1200) js FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 12")
for r in rows: print(json.dumps(r))

# ledger
for window,name in [(7,'last7'),(14,'last14'),(28,'last28')]:
    rows=q(f"""SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-window} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC""")
    print_rows(f'ledger {name}', rows, 50)
rows=q(f"SELECT day,category,SUM(amount) amt,COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-7} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY day,category ORDER BY day,category")
print_rows('ledger by day last7', rows, 200)

# service
rows=q(f"SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day>{DAY-14} ORDER BY day")
print_rows('service last14', rows, 30)

# active book summary
rows=q("""SELECT c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed,ROUND(s.promotion,2) promo,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) eff,
              COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr,AVG(s.seat_count) avg_seats,
              MIN(s.start_day) min_start,MAX(s.start_day) max_start,MIN(s.billing_day_mod30) min_mod,MAX(s.billing_day_mod30) max_mod
       FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
       WHERE s.status='subscribed'
       GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2),ROUND(s.promotion,2),ROUND(s.effective_price,2)
       ORDER BY mrr DESC""")
print_rows('active book by type/group/plan/list/promo/eff', rows, 200)
print('\nTotals active', q("SELECT COUNT(*) subs,SUM(seat_count) seats,SUM(effective_price*seat_count) mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'"))

# active usage last week by plan/group; join on active current sub only (approx)
rows=q(f"""SELECT c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) users,SUM(du.usage_units) units,
       SUM(CASE s.plan WHEN 'A' THEN du.usage_units*0.006 WHEN 'B' THEN du.usage_units*0.012 WHEN 'C' THEN du.usage_units*0.03 ELSE 0 END) est_compute_current_tiers,
       SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count)/COUNT(DISTINCT s.customer_id) avg_mrr_per_sub
       FROM daily_usage du JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=du.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
       JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
       WHERE du.day>{DAY-7} AND du.day<={DAY}
       GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY units DESC""")
print_rows('usage last7 by active current group/plan (approx)', rows, 100)

# payments parse last week
pat=re.compile(r'customer (\d+)')
payments=[]
for d in range(DAY-6, DAY+1):
    for r in q(f"SELECT day, amount, note FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day={d}"):
        m=pat.search(r['note'] or '')
        if m: payments.append((d,int(m.group(1)),float(r['amount']),r['note']))
        else: payments.append((d,None,float(r['amount']),r['note']))
print('\nPayment rows last7',len(payments),'sum',sum(x[2] for x in payments),'missing cid',sum(1 for x in payments if x[1] is None))
ids=sorted({cid for _,cid,_,_ in payments if cid is not None})
subs={}
for i in range(0,len(ids),800):
    idstr=','.join(map(str,ids[i:i+800]))
    rows=q(f"""SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.status,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.promotion,s.effective_price,s.start_day,s.end_day,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30
              FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
              WHERE s.customer_id IN ({idstr})
              ORDER BY s.customer_id, CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, COALESCE(s.end_day,99999) DESC""")
    for r in rows: subs.setdefault(r['customer_id'],[]).append(r)
pay_groups=defaultdict(lambda:{'n':0,'amt':0.0,'seats':0})
for d,cid,amt,note in payments:
    rows=subs.get(cid,[])
    if not rows:
        key=('unknown','unknown','?',0,0,0,round(amt,2),'?')
        seats=0
    else:
        active=[r for r in rows if r['status']=='subscribed']
        r=active[0] if active else rows[0]
        age_pay=d-r['start_day'] if r['start_day'] is not None else None
        key=(r['customer_type'],r['group_id'],r['plan'],round(r['listed_price'],2),round(r['promotion'],2),round(r['effective_price'],2),round(amt,2),age_pay)
        seats=(r.get('seats') or r.get('seat_count') or 0)
    pay_groups[key]['n']+=1; pay_groups[key]['amt']+=amt; pay_groups[key]['seats']+=seats
print('\n## Payment groups top by n (type,group,plan,list,promo,eff,amt,age_pay)')
for key,val in sorted(pay_groups.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1]['n'], reverse=True)[:120]: print(key,val)
# build pay_age for renewal outcome
pay_age=defaultdict(lambda:{'paid_n':0,'paid_amt':0.0,'paid_seats':0})
for key,val in pay_groups.items():
    ctype,group,plan,listed,promo,eff,amt,age=key
    pay_age[(ctype,group,plan,listed,age)]['paid_n']+=val['n']; pay_age[(ctype,group,plan,listed,age)]['paid_amt']+=val['amt']; pay_age[(ctype,group,plan,listed,age)]['paid_seats']+=val['seats']
# cancellations/ lost last week status cancel/lost
cxl_rows=q(f"""SELECT c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed,(s.end_day-s.start_day) age_end,
                   COUNT(*) cxl_n,SUM(s.seat_count) cxl_seats,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) cxl_mrr, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,
                   GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ROUND(s.promotion,2)) promos
            FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
            WHERE s.status IN ('cancelled','lost') AND s.end_day>{DAY-7} AND s.end_day<={DAY}
            GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2),age_end""")
rows=[]
seen=set()
for r in cxl_rows:
    key=(r['customer_type'],r['group_id'],r['plan'],r['listed'],r['age_end'])
    seen.add(key)
    p=pay_age.get(key, {'paid_n':0,'paid_amt':0.0,'paid_seats':0})
    attempts=p['paid_n']+r['cxl_n']
    if attempts>=5:
        rows.append({**r,**p,'attempts':attempts,'churn_rate':r['cxl_n']/attempts if attempts else None})
for key,p in pay_age.items():
    if key not in seen and p['paid_n']>=5:
        rows.append({'customer_type':key[0],'group_id':key[1],'plan':key[2],'listed':key[3],'age_end':key[4],'cxl_n':0,'cxl_seats':0,'cxl_mrr':0,'avg_eff':None,'promos':'','paid_n':p['paid_n'],'paid_amt':p['paid_amt'],'paid_seats':p['paid_seats'],'attempts':p['paid_n'],'churn_rate':0.0})
print_rows('Renewal outcome last7 by type/group/plan/listed/age attempts>=5 sorted attempts', sorted(rows,key=lambda r:r['attempts'],reverse=True), 200)
print_rows('Renewal outcome last7 worst churn attempts>=20', sorted([r for r in rows if r['attempts']>=20],key=lambda r:r['churn_rate'],reverse=True), 100)
# cancellations totals
rows=q(f"""SELECT c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed,ROUND(s.promotion,2) promo,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) eff,COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) lost_mrr,MIN(s.end_day) min_end,MAX(s.end_day) max_end
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status IN ('cancelled','lost') AND s.end_day>{DAY-7} AND s.end_day<={DAY}
GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2),ROUND(s.promotion,2),ROUND(s.effective_price,2) ORDER BY lost_mrr DESC""")
print_rows('cancellations/lost last7 totals', rows, 100)

# due next weeks by active subscriptions: next 7, 28, remaining 70 days
def mods_for(n):
    return sorted({(DAY+i)%30 for i in range(1,n+1)})
for horizon in [7,14,28,70]:
    mods=mods_for(horizon)
    modlist=','.join(map(str,mods))
    rows=q(f"""SELECT c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2) listed,ROUND(s.promotion,2) promo,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) eff,COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr,MIN({DAY}-s.start_day) min_age,MAX({DAY}-s.start_day) max_age
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist})
    GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.listed_price,2),ROUND(s.promotion,2),ROUND(s.effective_price,2)
    ORDER BY mrr DESC""")
    print_rows(f'due active next {horizon}d mods {mods}', rows, 120)
    print('TOTAL due', horizon, q(f"SELECT COUNT(*) n,SUM(seat_count) seats,SUM(effective_price*seat_count) mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist})"))

# billing due by exact upcoming day
mods=mods_for(30)
rows=q(f"""SELECT ((s.billing_day_mod30-{DAY%30}+30)%30) days_ahead, c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) eff,COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr,MIN({DAY}-s.start_day) min_age,MAX({DAY}-s.start_day) max_age
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY days_ahead,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) HAVING days_ahead>0 ORDER BY days_ahead,mrr DESC""")
print_rows('due by exact days_ahead (1..29)', rows, 220)

# issues
rows=q(f"""SELECT i.group_id,i.days_open,COUNT(*) n FROM issues i WHERE i.status='open' GROUP BY i.group_id,i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id,i.days_open""")
print_rows('open issues by group/days_open', rows, 200)
rows=q(f"""SELECT i.group_id,COUNT(*) n,AVG(i.days_open) avg_age,MAX(i.days_open) max_age,
SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_sub_issues
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open' GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC""")
print_rows('open issues summary', rows, 100)

# enterprise threads
rows=q("""WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT e.thread_type,e.sender,e.turn_number,e.day,COUNT(*) threads,SUM(e.seat_count) seats,AVG(e.seat_count) avg_seats,MIN(e.seat_count) min_seats,MAX(e.seat_count) max_seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns e ON e.message_id=l.mid GROUP BY e.thread_type,e.sender,e.turn_number,e.day ORDER BY e.day DESC, threads DESC""")
print_rows('enterprise latest open threads counts by type/sender/turn/day', rows, 200)
rows=q("""WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT e.message_id,e.customer_id,e.thread_type,e.turn_number,e.sender,e.day,e.seat_count,e.email,c.group_id,substr(e.message_text,1,500) msg,e.offer_json
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns e ON e.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=e.customer_id
WHERE e.sender!='agent' ORDER BY e.day DESC,e.seat_count DESC LIMIT 80""")
print_rows('enterprise latest open non-agent sample', rows, 100)
rows=q("""SELECT c.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.effective_price,s.start_day,s.seat_count,s.billing_day_mod30,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enterprise_turns e WHERE e.customer_id=c.customer_id AND e.closed=0) open_threads
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='large' ORDER BY s.seat_count DESC""")
print_rows('active enterprise subscriptions', rows, 100)
# closed accepted recent
rows=q(f"""SELECT e.day,e.thread_type,COUNT(*) n,SUM(e.seat_count) seats,MIN(e.seat_count) min_seats,MAX(e.seat_count) max_seats
FROM enterprise_turns e WHERE e.closed=1 AND e.close_reason='accepted' AND e.day>{DAY-14} GROUP BY e.day,e.thread_type ORDER BY e.day DESC""")
print_rows('enterprise accepted closes last14', rows, 100)
